content:
Goal: Create a wide horizontal triptych showing the same fantasy sky-kingdom scene rendered in three progressive art stages, like a concept art evolution from rough sketch to finished illustration.

Canvas: Very wide 3-panel image, approximately 2:1 aspect ratio, divided into exactly 3 equal vertical panels with no visible borders beyond the panel edges.

Layout: Each panel shows the same composition from a dramatic low, tilted perspective: a young fantasy knight stands in the lower foreground on a stone bridge or stairway, looking toward the viewer, holding or surrounded by a long flowing pale mint cloak that streams leftward in the wind. Behind the knight is a grand floating castle city suspended in a bright blue sky, with cables, stone columns, ruins, greenery, and clouds creating strong depth and motion lines.

Panel count and descriptions: Use exactly 3 panels. Panel 1, left: loose rough concept sketch with pale blue sky wash, visible construction lines, scribbly black pencil strokes, unfinished architecture, motion streaks, and minimal muted color. Panel 2, center: clean anime-style colored illustration with strong ink outlines, cel-shaded armor, saturated blue sky, fluffy clouds, green terraces, stone bridge details, and a floating castle in the distance. Panel 3, right: highly polished final fantasy illustration with sparkling sunlight, dense architectural detail, vivid blue sky, bright white clouds, lush green vines and trees, glowing highlights, painterly rendering, and cinematic clarity.

Subject details: The foreground character is {argument name="character type" default="a young blond fantasy knight"}, wearing ornate silver-and-gold armor with leather straps, boots, gloves, and a large flowing pale mint-white cape. The face may be softly simplified or obscured, but the body should face forward in a heroic pose with the cape sweeping dramatically across the scene. Keep the character consistently placed in all 3 panels: lower center foreground, slightly larger in the right panel, with the same costume and pose.

Environment details: The setting is {argument name="fantasy location" default="a floating castle city above the clouds"}. Include suspended stone platforms, carved pillars, bridge railings, hanging cables or ropes stretching diagonally across the sky, fragments of ancient ruins, patches of green grass, ivy, trees, and many white clouds. The background castle should be tall, ornate, vertical, and luminous, with towers and terraces stacked upward.

Visual style: Japanese fantasy anime concept art, dynamic perspective, wind-swept movement, adventurous mood, luminous sky palette. Main colors are {argument name="main color palette" default="sky blue, cloud white, pale mint, stone gray, gold, and fresh green"}. The right panel should feel magical and camera-facing with sparkling highlights and refined detail, while the left panel remains visibly unfinished.

Constraints: No captions, no logos, no watermarks, no text overlays. Maintain exactly 3 vertical stages of the same scene. Emphasize the progression from rough sketch to clean color to final polished fantasy key art.
title: Fantasy Sky Castle Art Evolution Triptych
description: A three-panel fantasy concept art progression showing a knight before a floating castle, useful for generating comparison-style illustration stages.

Layout and exact elements: On the far left, place a huge navy title reading {argument name="character name" default="KIKI"}. Under it, include three text blocks: ROLE: Dreamer / Explorer; AGE: 16; PERSONALITY: Curious, playful, a little clumsy, full of wonder. Silhouettes and color palette: In the lower center, include a SILHOUETTES section with exactly three dark navy full-body silhouettes: straight front pose, walking side pose, and playful pose holding the cat bag. Next to it, include a COLOR PALETTE section with exactly nine oval swatches arranged in a 3x3 grid: pink, lavender, purple, periwinkle blue, peach cream, pale peach, deep navy, mauve rose, and coral pink.

content:
Goal: Create a clean isometric vector illustration sheet showing exactly 21 separate business-person poses and office mini-scenes on one canvas, like a reusable character asset collection.

Canvas: Wide 16:9 horizontal canvas, light gray or off-white background, no borders, no title text, no labels, no watermark. Arrange the subjects in a neat 3-row by 7-column grid with generous spacing and soft gray oval shadows under each figure or desk.

Visual style: Modern isometric corporate illustration, semi-flat vector rendering with subtle gradients and soft shading, crisp edges, realistic but simplified proportions, neutral professional mood. Use {argument name="background color" default="very light gray"}. Characters wear {argument name="business outfit style" default="white shirts, navy skirts or trousers, dark shoes, simple ties for men"}; women have brown hair tied back, men have short brown hair. Use a restrained palette of white, navy, charcoal, brown wood, gray metal, and black shoes.

Layout and exact scene count: Include exactly 21 discrete illustrations, arranged left to right in three rows of seven.

Top row, 7 scenes: 1) standing businesswoman holding a dark tablet or clipboard, 2) businesswoman seen from behind writing on a freestanding whiteboard with a marker, 3) businesswoman walking while holding a takeaway coffee cup, 4) businesswoman standing and working on an open laptop, 5) businesswoman standing with one hand to her ear as if talking on a phone, 6) businesswoman standing and writing on a black clipboard, 7) businesswoman seated at a wooden desk using a laptop on an office chair.

Middle row, 7 scenes: 1) businessman standing with a black briefcase at his side, 2) two businessmen shaking hands, 3) businessman walking while reading paper documents, 4) businessman leaning over a wooden desk and typing on a laptop, 5) businessman sitting in a black office chair with crossed legs and one hand on his chin, 6) businessman standing and talking on a phone, 7) businessman standing casually with hands in pockets.

Bottom row, 7 scenes: 1) businesswoman hurrying or running while holding a tablet and stylus, 2) businessman placing or retrieving binders from a tall gray office shelf, 3) businessman celebrating with both fists raised, 4) businesswoman sitting casually on the edge of a wooden desk while holding a takeaway coffee, 5) businesswoman seated at a wooden desk using a laptop, 6) businessman sitting in a dark lounge chair thinking with one hand near his chin, 7) businesswoman standing and talking on a phone while holding a blue folder.

Subject details: Keep all figures small, full-body, three-quarter isometric view with consistent scale. Desks are simple brown tabletops with thin gray legs; chairs are dark office chairs where appropriate; whiteboard has a thin gray frame and legs; shelf is gray with visible binders. Emphasize office work gestures, communication, meetings, walking, thinking, and productivity. Use {argument name="character hair color" default="brown"} and {argument name="primary accent color" default="navy blue"}.

Constraints: No text anywhere in the image, no logos, no photorealism, no exaggerated cartoon faces, no background environment beyond the individual props and shadows. Keep the composition airy, evenly spaced, and suitable for business presentation assets.

Main subject: On the right half, show one elegant young anime character with long white-silver hair in two thick braids, soft cat ears, and a fluffy white tail. content:
Goal: Create a richly detailed fantasy concept art scene of a floating night market above the clouds, blending steampunk airships, sky docks, and colorful bazaar tents.

Canvas: Vertical 2:3 composition, cinematic wide-angle view, high fantasy digital painting with painterly realism, deep perspective, dramatic scale, no text or watermark.

Setting and atmosphere: A vast aerial marketplace suspended among thick white clouds at twilight or night. The sky is dark navy and stormy overhead, with heavy rolling clouds, while a pale glowing horizon cuts across the middle distance. The mood should feel magical, bustling, and slightly mysterious, with warm lantern light contrasting against cool blue moonlit clouds.

Layout: Foreground and lower half filled with wooden market platforms, bridges, railings, stalls, hanging fabric canopies, and crowds. Midground opens into a cloud-filled gap with more floating docks and ships. Background shows distant clouds and flying vessels receding into haze.

Counted major elements: Include exactly 3 visible airships: 1 large oval zeppelin-like ship on the upper left with a dark wooden gondola and many rigging cables, 1 smaller distant airship centered in the background, and 1 very large balloon-and-gondola airship docked on the upper right with a round vertical envelope, ropes, masts, and a projecting gangway. Include exactly 5 main market areas: 1 lower-left stall cluster beneath dark blue striped canvas, 1 center-lower wooden dock with lanterns and figures, 1 right-lower covered bazaar with striped awnings and food stalls, 1 middle-left floating market platform in the clouds, and 1 upper-right docked platform beside the large airship. Include many small human figures as a dense crowd of merchants and shoppers, but keep them tiny and atmospheric rather than portrait-like.

Subject details: Wooden floating piers should appear suspended in the sky with no visible ground, supported by fantasy engineering and partly hidden by clouds. Stalls sell spices, fruits, fabrics, trinkets, baskets, and glowing objects. Add draped cloth awnings in deep blue, teal, red, orange, purple, and gold. Use numerous warm hanging lanterns and street lamps throughout the scene, especially along railings and stall fronts. content:
Goal: Create a detailed vertical fantasy character sheet for {argument name="character name" default="Arpeggio (アルペジオ)"}, a humanized instrument spirit based on {argument name="instrument name" default="Wishbass 11 String Fanned Fret Bass"}. The sheet should look like a premium illustrated game-design reference page with sepia parchment paper, thin dark brown borders, elegant serif typography, and Japanese-inspired layout styling.

Canvas: Portrait orientation, approximately 3:4 ratio, aged cream paper background with a thin double-line frame and small corner ornaments. Use warm browns, walnut wood tones, black leather, muted red accents, and gold highlights. The overall style is painterly anime / fantasy concept art, highly detailed, refined, and print-ready.

Main subject: A tall androgynous young male character, early twenties, 182 cm impression, standing full-body on the left-center of the page while holding an enormous multi-string bass guitar upright like a sacred artifact. He has long messy {argument name="hair color" default="chestnut brown hair with reddish streaks"}, layered strands, loose waves, and a mysterious soft-blurred facial area with no detailed facial features. Outfit: sleeveless dark open vest, layered necklaces, leather wrist wraps, fingerless gloves, asymmetric belts, flowing dark cloth strips, black trousers, high boots, and wooden instrument-like armor panels. The character is calm, poetic, and solitary, as if listening to the resonance of wood. The bass is oversized, with 11 visible strings, fanned frets, a natural multi-tone wood body, dark fretboard, tuning pegs along an elongated headstock, and a rich luthier-crafted look.

Layout: Top left contains a large elegant title reading Arpeggio, with a smaller subtitle naming the instrument and a character sheet label. Down the left side beside the character, place a vertical poetic tagline in Japanese-style arrangement meaning: resonance connects, sound tells stories. Right side contains three stacked information panels. Bottom third contains expression portraits, standing-pose variations, notes, logo, and color palette.

Right-side panels: Include exactly 3 boxed panels. Panel 1 is Basic Information with 8 rows: name, species, gender, age, height, birthday, affiliation, and role. Panel 2 is Profile with a paragraph describing a spirit born from the Wishbass instrument, quiet and introverted, speaking through resonance and music. Panel 3 is Instrument Data with front and side views of the bass plus technical bullet points and a small tonal-range meter.

Instrument data details: Show exactly 2 instrument diagrams in the panel: one front view and one thin side view. Add concise specs: 11 strings, fanned fret scale, multi-wood body, through-neck or laminated neck construction, custom pickup, wide range, clear low tones, rich harmonics, and unique wood resonance. Include one horizontal tone-image bar labeled from low to high, with warm brown-to-gold coloring.

Lower sections: Include exactly 4 expression bust portraits in a row, each with the same blurred facial area and hair/outfit consistency. Label them as: normal, faint smile, performing, serious. To the right, include exactly 2 standing variation miniatures: stage outfit holding the giant bass dynamically, and casual artisan/workshop outfit standing with the bass. At bottom left, include a related settings / notes box with short lore bullets. At bottom center, include a simple Wishbass logo mark with a large W and small leather-and-bass-design text. At bottom right, include exactly 5 color swatches labeled by motif: walnut, maple, padauk, dark brown, and gold.

Text style: The page may use Japanese-style headings and compact annotation blocks, but keep the visible design clean and legible. Use elegant serif Latin text for the main title and small neat Japanese-style body text blocks for authenticity. Do not overcrowd the sheet beyond the described panels.

Constraints: Exactly 1 main full-body character, exactly 1 oversized 11-string bass held by the character, exactly 3 right-side information panels, exactly 2 instrument diagrams, exactly 4 expression portraits, exactly 2 standing variation miniatures, and exactly 5 color swatches. No modern UI, no sci-fi neon, no photographic realism, no watermark, no extra characters.
title: Arpeggio Bass Humanization Sheet
description: A richly detailed fantasy character sheet for a humanized 11-string fanned-fret bass spirit, useful for concept art and original character design.

content:
Goal: Create an ultra-detailed vertical cyberpunk architectural anime illustration of an unfinished giant girl embedded inside a futuristic megastructure construction site, with the mood of “unfinished girl.”

Canvas: Vertical 2:3 poster composition, dense full-frame illustration, white paper background with fine black ink linework and watercolor-like color accents. Bottom-right has a small handwritten date and signature reading “May 29, 2026” and “Oyagi.”

Main subject: Show exactly 1 colossal anime girl at the center, partially constructed or partially hidden by surrounding architecture. She has {argument name="hair color" default="dark black hair with teal-green streaks"}, short messy hair falling across her neck, pale skin, and a loose white draped garment over her shoulders and torso. Her head and upper body are integrated into the city-scale scaffolding, making her look like a giant incomplete statue or living building. Place exactly 1 opaque square censor/placeholder block over her face, centered in the upper middle of the image; the block is a muted gray-brown gradient rectangle with sharp edges and no text.

Environment: A hyper-complex futuristic construction city packed with white modular towers, exposed steel scaffolding, catwalks, staircases, ladders, cranes, maintenance decks, capsule rooms, glass elevators, gantries, railings, tiny workers, and industrial platforms. The architecture should feel like a cross between a high-tech refinery, a modular space station, and a dense Japanese construction-site city.

Counted prominent elements: Include exactly 1 central giant girl, exactly 1 face-covering square block, exactly 4 dominant crane/gantry arms crossing the composition, exactly 5 large bright magenta industrial pipe runs, exactly 3 large transparent capsule/elevator pods, exactly 1 yellow circular tunnel ring in the lower-left center, and exactly 1 small rooftop house-like module with a yellow-and-white roof in the lower-right foreground. Add numerous smaller pipes, decks, miniature workers, and structural details as background texture, but keep the listed prominent elements visually readable.

Color palette: Mostly white and black technical drawing lines with vivid accent colors: hot magenta pipes, cyan and teal tubes, yellow railings and modules, orange crane beams, green structural braces, small blue panels, and occasional red details. Keep the girl's clothing mostly white so she blends into the architecture.

Style: Extremely intricate hand-drawn anime architectural concept art, fine ink outlines, meticulous perspective, dense mechanical detail, crisp line art, subtle watercolor wash, high-resolution poster illustration, inspired by futuristic Japanese sci-fi cityscapes and construction diagrams. No photorealism, no 3D render look.

Composition: The girl’s torso occupies the center-left to center area, surrounded and overlapped by platforms and pipes. The upper half is crowded with towers and scaffolding. The lower foreground contains walkways, railings, capsule structures, and tiny people for scale. Use strong vertical depth and layered perspective so the city feels enormous and crowded.

Constraints: No large readable headline text, no extra logos, no UI, no border. Keep the face block intentionally blank and opaque. Maintain the “unfinished construction” feeling and make the image overwhelmingly detailed.
title: Unfinished Girl Megastructure
description: A dense cyberpunk anime construction-site city illustration featuring a giant unfinished girl embedded in scaffolding and industrial architecture.

content:
Use the reference image as the {argument name="character description" default="base for character design"} and depict a "moment living in a world of handmade clay animation."

Maintain the character's face, hairstyle, hair color, eyes, ears, costume, and facial expression impression.

Never break the character's identity.

Maintain the atmosphere of the character as an anime illustration.

Do not turn into a real person.

The character itself should be depicted as a being with an atmosphere like a "handmade clay doll," "protagonist of a stop-motion animation," "resident of clay work," or "character of a handmade clay animation."

However, do not make it a toy itself. Do not make the texture like a mass-produced plastic figure. Do not make it a display photo of a realistic clay work. Do not make it look like a 3DCG animation. Do not make it look like a resin figure.

The character exists as a resident living in a clay animation world. The stage is a warm and cozy {argument name="world view" default="clay animation world"} that matches the character in the reference image.

Buildings, furniture, plants, tableware, small items, and daily necessities are all constructed as a clay animation set made by hand. The entire space should have a sense of handmade quality, warmth, friendliness, and a gentle sense of daily life characteristic of stop-motion animation works.

The character is naturally {argument name="action" default="living"} in that space. Performing natural actions that feel like a peaceful daily life, such as reading a book, cooking, taking care of plants, drinking tea, cleaning, resting by the window, or relaxing with a blanket.

Scale between furniture, architecture, plants, small items, daily necessities, and characters should be naturally unified. Do not create a composition where a giant person appears to have entered a miniature space. Do not make only the character appear life-sized. Depict the entire world as being unified at the same scale as the clay animation world.

Soft shapes that look like they were carefully molded with fingers. Slight distortions. Outlines that are not too perfect. Incorporate the warmth of handiwork and the kindness unique to handmade works. Naturally incorporate a sense of craft.

Gentle sense of daily life. Atmosphere like a small village. Sense of security like a stop-motion work for children. Quiet daily storytelling. Atmospheric production that feels seasonal. Warm lighting. Soft colors. Friendly background composition. Depth like a handmade clay animation work.

The background should be designed with the highest priority of making people feel that "I want to live in this clay animation world."

Horror, eerie, dark fantasy, or grotesque expressions are prohibited. Game UI, HUD, menus, status bars, and interface displays are prohibited.
title: Handmade Claymation Style i2i
description: An image-to-image prompt to transform reference characters into a cozy, handmade claymation style typical of stop-motion animation.

content:
Goal: Create a dark fantasy worldbuilding map for a gothic horror board game or campaign setting, showing a haunted swamp realm at night with branching travel routes.

Canvas: Wide 16:9 illustrated map, antique parchment border, muted blue-green-gray palette, high-detail painterly fantasy concept art. The scene is viewed from above at a slight angle, like an illustrated adventure map.

Layout: Fill the entire canvas with a misty marshland, twisted forests, bog water, ruins, and supernatural landmarks. Add an ornate thin black frame with delicate Victorian filigree corners, skull ornaments in the upper-left and upper-right corners, and a compass rose in the lower-left corner. Use moonlit lighting, fog layers, and small glowing cyan, violet, and ghostly green accents.

Map route system: Draw exactly 34 connected circular route nodes across the map: 28 blank parchment oval nodes and 6 special illustrated medallion nodes. Connect the nodes with pale winding paths that snake through the marsh. The 6 special medallions should show, from left/center to right/bottom: a feather symbol, a skull symbol, a bat-or-crest symbol, an open book symbol, a target-like spiral symbol, and a dead tree symbol. Keep the nodes empty except for these icons; no numbers.

Named location banners: Include exactly 5 curled parchment ribbon labels with readable Portuguese fantasy names: “{argument name="western gate label" default="PORTÃO ESQUECIDO"}” near the left haunted gate, “{argument name="upper right crypt label" default="CRIPTA DOS LAMENTOS"}” near the ruined crypt at the upper right, “{argument name="eastern ruins label" default="SAUDAÇÕES DA NÉVOA"}” near the right-side ruins, “{argument name="lower right lighthouse label" default="FAROL DA ALMA PERDIDA"}” near the lower-right lighthouse, and “{argument name="lower left dock label" default="ATRACADOURO SOMBRIO"}” near the lower-left dock.

Landmark details: In the upper left, place a distant black gothic castle with many spires beneath a bright full moon and storm clouds. At far left, show an iron cemetery gate glowing faint cyan. In the upper right, show a crumbling mausoleum or crypt with a pale statue in the doorway. At right-center, show jagged ghostly turquoise crystal formations and ruined stones. At lower right, show a lonely crooked lighthouse glowing purple through fog. At lower left, show a rotting wooden dock over dark water with a cyan lantern. Near the center, include a flooded obelisk island, dead trees, swamp pools, a ruined windmill, ghostly figures, and scattered grave markers.

Visual style: Grim, atmospheric, hand-painted, intricate, old-world fantasy cartography mixed with gothic illustration. Use desaturated colors, heavy mist, moonlight, painterly brushwork, and subtle paper texture. Avoid modern UI elements, bright colors, clean vector art, or extra labels beyond the 5 banners.
title: Gothic Haunted Swamp Map
description: Creates a detailed dark fantasy adventure map with haunted landmarks, branching route nodes, and Portuguese parchment labels for worldbuilding or tabletop campaigns.

content:
Female announcers, etc.
A high-quality image of a female TV announcer introducing a "popular robot toy" in a live TV broadcast style inside a large, bright toy store.
The setting is a large toy store. The store is filled with toy shelves, product packaging, colorful POP displays, aisles, bright lighting, and a lively sales floor atmosphere. The background has a realistic and natural commercial facility look.
IMPORTANT: 
Do not show the robot itself in the frame.
However, the dedicated large display shelves, stands, or dedicated spaces for the robot must be clearly and firmly visible in the frame.
The display space should be large enough to place a robot later and be in a prominent position.
Around the display shelves and stands, place promotional panels, product boxes, related goods, POPs, and description boards that evoke words like "the hottest new robot," "hit product," and "featured exhibit" so that it is immediately clear this is a robot sales/display corner.
However, do not show the overall image or the machine body of the robot itself.
The female announcer is a friendly and neat TV reporter. She is talking to the camera with a bright smile. She is wearing elegant and natural clothing for news reporting. She may be holding a microphone or interview cards. The atmosphere is like a news or information program, introducing the shop while explaining the hot product.
The scene depicts her saying "this robot is so popular it's flying off the shelves." In the background shelves and sales areas, related toy boxes, posters, and promotional panels are lined up to convey that it is a popular product. In particular, the robot display shelves, stands, and empty spaces should be clearly shown, creating a composition where the later presence or departure of a robot feels natural.
Dialogue style:
"Reporting live from the toy department!"
"The hottest thing drawing attention right now is this popular new robot toy!"
"Its popularity is incredible—it's selling like hotcakes!"
"It's attracting huge interest here at the display corner as well!"
"It's the hottest item right now, drawing attention from children to adults alike!"
Composition points:
Place the announcer in the foreground or midground, ensuring the robot display shelves, stands, and dedicated space are clearly visible in the background.
The shelves and stands should have enough presence in the frame so they don't look like there's no room to place anything.
Identify the robot sales/display area clearly through empty display stands, wide shelves, dedicated sections, and surrounding related products.
Three-view (Robot):
A high-quality three-view design sheet for an original popular robot. A clean, setting-material-style layout showing front, side, and back views aligned. White or light gray background. No extra flourishes; the shape of the machine should be clearly visible.
The robot is a sharp, sophisticated near-future mecha design in the style of Virtual-On. It has a slim, high-mobility, linear, and stylish silhouette, aerodynamic armor lines, sharp legs, a flowing body shape, and large vernier units or high-mobility thrusters on the back. Strongly convey the impression of being skilled in high-speed and aerial maneuvers. Overall, a mecha with a sense of speed like those in arcade mecha games.
IMPORTANT:
Create a completely original design that does not look too much like a Gundam.
Avoid the following features:
- V-shaped antennas
- Typical Gundam faces
- Mouth-area face slits
- Tricolor-based color schemes that evoke strong déjà vu
- Classic heroic protagonist symbols used as-is
- Configurations where the combination of a boxy chest and face looks like an existing robot work
Instead, design in the following direction:
- The head is a slender sensor head or a horizontal visor type
- The face is not too human-like, but mechanical and futuristic
- Short antennas, sensor protrusions, or streamlined blade motifs on the head
- Slim, high-density armor configuration for the chest
- Shoulders are sharp and functional, not excessively large
- Arms and legs are slim but powerful, prioritizing mobility
- Large thrusters/verniers on the back stand out as key features of the machine
- The feet are smart shapes prioritizing high mobility over grounded stability
The color scheme should be a stylish, futuristic original coloring, combining colors like white-gray, blue-gray, black, silver, purple, turquoise, and orange. The design should look persuasive as a serious mecha popular enough to be commercialized, rather than just a toy.
Carefully render panel lines, joints, overlapping armor, sensors, thrusters, and details. However, prioritize a light and agile impression over heavy armor.
The main goal is for the front, side, and back designs to be clearly understood. Overall, complete it as a three-view drawing of a high-mobility original near-future mecha capable of taking flight into the sky.
Do not include unnecessary numbers, long explanations, storyboard elements, or handwritten notes on the screen. If necessary, keep it to a small, natural live-TV-style ticker. Overall, create a single image in a bright, natural news report style where the announcer and the robot display space are easy to recognize.
title: News Reporter Toy Store Scene
description: A prompt for generating a scene where a female news anchor reports on a popular robot toy from within a bright, busy toy shop.

content:
Goal: Create a wide cinematic fantasy adventure storyboard sheet showing a single continuous action sequence starring {argument name="character name" default="a tiny child adventurer"} in a vast enchanted mushroom forest.

Canvas: Horizontal 16:9 image, one illustration divided into exactly 12 storyboard panels arranged in a 4-column by 3-row grid. Use thin black borders between panels. Place a small white cut number in the upper-left corner of every panel, numbered exactly 1 through 12.

Visual style: Painterly high-detail fantasy concept art with a Ghibli-like sense of wonder, dynamic cinematic compositions, soft dappled sunlight, atmospheric depth, swirling spores, vines, branches, moss, oversized mushrooms, warm orange and cool blue color contrast, visible brush texture, adventurous and whimsical mood.

Main subject: A small wide-eyed child adventurer wearing {argument name="cloak color" default="a bright blue hooded cloak"}, rugged boots, and a large woven basket backpack filled with acorns, berries, leaves, and forest trinkets. The child carries or discovers {argument name="magical object" default="a glowing purple mushroom"}. The scale should make the child look tiny among gigantic mushrooms and forest plants.

Panel sequence: Include exactly 12 discrete panels with these numbered actions:
1. Rear view of the hooded child climbing across an enormous orange-spotted mushroom cap, basket backpack visible, surrounded by towering mushrooms and forest debris.
2. Close cinematic view from under foliage as the child peeks over a huge blue mushroom cap, curious face visible, soft light filtering through leaves.
3. The child reaches toward a glowing purple mushroom nestled among roots, acorns, vines, and tiny plants.
4. Close-up of the child carefully lifting or holding the glowing purple mushroom with both hands, face full of wonder.
5. The child crouches near the giant blue mushroom while the purple mushroom glows brightly in the foreground, branches and oversized fungi framing the scene.
6. Full-body action shot of the child running across a large orange mushroom cap, basket bouncing, adventurous expression.
7. Extreme close-up of one boot landing hard on the mushroom cap, kicking up spores, dust, and textured debris.
8. Dynamic midair leap between giant mushrooms, the child clutching the glowing purple mushroom while vines and branches sweep past.
9. The child swings through the forest on a thick vine or rope, one boot forward, basket backpack visible, dramatic motion and depth.
10. Wide shot of the child running away across a narrow wooden suspension bridge high in the mushroom forest canopy.
11. Long shot of the child standing triumphantly on a huge orange mushroom cap above the forest, small against a sweeping magical landscape.
12. Final close/medium shot of the child seated or resting on an orange-spotted mushroom cap, looking back toward the viewer, holding the glowing purple mushroom, with open sky and a distant enchanted forest behind.

Constraints: Keep the storyboard as one coherent image, not 12 separate files. Maintain consistent character, outfit, backpack, and magical object across all panels. No speech bubbles, no captions besides the white panel numbers, no logos, no watermark.
title: Fantasy Mushroom Forest Storyboard
description: Generates a 12-panel cinematic fantasy adventure storyboard featuring a tiny hooded child exploring an enchanted mushroom forest.

content: The attached images are for identity reference. Do not be overly influenced by the art style, coloring, line work, proportions, shading, staging, original costume, original pose, body orientation, hand position, leg placement, gaze direction, camera angle, or composition of the reference images. Maintain the {argument name="character traits" default="facial features, hairstyle, hair color, eyes, perceived age, body type, atmosphere, personality impression, motif, charm, symbolic details, and species"}. Design a new "Taisho Roman x Modern Fashion" mixed outfit that best suits this character. The goal is not to simply dress them in Taisho-era clothing, but to reimagine what they would wear if they lived in the modern day while naturally incorporating the aesthetics and eclectic atmosphere of the Taisho era. Do not perform a simple transformation of the original outfit; extract hints of colors or motifs only as needed. Aim for a balanced direction of approximately {argument name="mix balance" default="50% Taisho Roman and 50% Modern Fashion"}. Use modern clothing as a base while ensuring that the elegance, retro feel, and eclectic Japanese-Western style of the Taisho era are clearly conveyed. However, do not make it a historical reproduction or Japanese cosplay; establish it as sophisticated "Taisho Modern" fashion that can be worn naturally today. The outfit should balance "Taisho Roman elegance, retro feel, and eclectic style" with "modern fashion's sophistication, effortless chic, practicality, and trendiness." Avoid simply adding Japanese patterns to ordinary modern clothes, and conversely, avoid over-reproduction of hakama or kimono. Create a design where both elements blend naturally. Optimize the outfit composition, patterns, color schemes, accessories, and textures by appropriately choosing from styles such as neat, lovely, intellectual, decadent, glamorous, cool, active, androgynous, bewitching, ephemeral, or dignified, according to the character's impression. Permitted elements: Haori-style outerwear, kimono-sleeve details, Japanese coat details, hakama-style bottoms, pleated skirts, long skirts, wide pants, blouses, shirts, dresses, sets, jackets, short tops, standing collars, frills, ribbon ties, obi-style belts, corset-style waist marks, Japanese patterns, floral patterns, geometric patterns, lace, lace-up boots, leather shoes, platforms, pumps, hats, glasses, gloves, clasp bags, mini bags, shoulder bags, umbrellas, hair ornaments, tassels, antique items, and modern accessories. Do not fix the clothing category; choose naturally according to the character's gender impression, build, perceived age, personality, role, and motif. Maintain modern silhouettes, layering, ease of wear, and urban sophistication while clearly reflecting Taisho Roman-derived details and atmosphere. Avoid a cosplay feel and establish it as natural, sophisticated "modern clothing with a Taisho Roman taste." Choose a completely new pose, angle, composition, and expression different from the original image. Using the same standing posture, hand position, facial orientation, center of gravity, or silhouette as the original image is prohibited. Ensure the pose is natural, showcases the outfit well, and conveys the character's personality. Choose a background space that naturally suits the character. Use modern urban spaces, interiors, shops, cafes, or street corners as a base, with a subtle hint of Taisho Roman design or retro atmosphere. The background should not be too prominent and should enhance the character and outfit. Reconstruct the final output into a single unified completed art style. {argument name="visual style" default="High-quality commercial anime illustration style, sharp lines, clean skin, transparent eyes, well-groomed hair, elegant fabric textures, natural lighting"}. No text, no logos, no noise.
title: Taisho Roman x Modern Fashion Fusion
description: A detailed instruction set for creating character designs that blend 1920s Japanese Taisho-era aesthetics with contemporary modern fashion, focusing on maintaining character identity from reference images.

content:
Goal: Create a clean anime concept art reference sheet for a {argument name="character name" default="Samurai Mecha Girl"}, combining a school-uniform samurai heroine with exposed light-mecha prosthetic limbs, presented as high-resolution monochrome production-design line art with small technical notes.

Canvas: Vertical portrait character sheet, off-white paper background, thin black outer border, 3:4 aspect ratio. Use crisp black and gray pencil/ink linework, minimal shading, no full color except a small color guide. The overall look is a Japanese setting-materials sheet, precise and highly detailed.

Layout: Top-left large English title “Samurai Mecha Girl” with smaller Japanese subtitle “侍機巧娘” below it. The left and center two-thirds contain the main full-body character illustration. The right third is a boxed head-turnaround panel. The bottom area contains weapon and mechanical-part diagrams. Keep the page airy, technical, and neatly ruled with thin divider lines.

Main subject: A 17-year-old anime girl with {argument name="hair style" default="short straight bob hair with blunt bangs"}, serious eyes, and circular mechanical ear/head sensor modules. She wears a sailor-style school uniform with scarf, short pleated skirt, thigh-high stockings, and mechanical armor integrated into one arm and one leg. Pose her kneeling dynamically: one knee down, one cybernetic leg raised, torso angled forward, one mechanical hand braced near the ground, and the other hand holding a katana horizontally above her head. The katana has a wrapped handle and a long slightly curved blade. Her mecha limbs are detailed with pistons, circular joints, armor plates, cables, segmented fingers, and heavy boot components.

Visible text and labeled sections: Include exactly 7 major text/detail blocks: 1) top-left title block with “Samurai Mecha Girl” and “侍機巧娘”; 2) a stats box listing “TYPE : ASSAULT”, “CLASS : LIGHT MECHA”, “HEIGHT : 158cm”, “WEIGHT : 49kg”, “AGE : 17”, “AFFILIATION : UNKNOWN”; 3) a “CONCEPT” block with short Japanese paragraph lines; 4) a “SPEC” block with Japanese bullet points; 5) a bottom weapon section labeled “武装” and “ENERGY KATANA / エネルギー式刀剣”; 6) a bottom mechanical parts section labeled “ARM UNIT / 腕部ユニット” and “LEG UNIT / 脚部ユニット”; 7) a right-side lower notes area with “COLOR GUIDE（参考）” and “DETAIL”.

Right-side head turnaround panel: Add one bordered panel titled “頭部ラフスケッチ三面図”. Inside it, show exactly 3 head drawings stacked vertically and labeled “FRONT”, “SIDE”, and “BACK”: front view with face and headband, side profile showing the circular ear mechanism, and back view showing the bob haircut and rear of the mechanical module.

Bottom diagrams: In the weapon box, show exactly 1 horizontal energy katana diagram with a wrapped grip. In the mechanical parts box, show exactly 5 separate component drawings: two arm-unit views on the left and three leg-unit views on the right, each rendered as detailed mechanical line-art studies.

Color guide: Include exactly 6 small square swatches with labels: main armor dark iron, sub armor white gray, accent red, mechanical joints gold, cloth dark blue, hair brown, plus a small orange eye/accent swatch if needed; keep these as the only colored elements on the page.

Style constraints: Use refined monochrome anime drafting, thin clean outlines, technical callout-sheet composition, high-detail mechanical design, subtle paper texture, sharp readable typography, no background scenery, no dramatic lighting, no painterly rendering, no extra characters.
title: Samurai Mecha Girl Reference Sheet
description: A detailed anime production reference sheet for a school-uniform samurai girl with mechanical limbs, weapon diagrams, head turnarounds, and technical notes.

content:
Goal: Create a cute anime character special expression sheet for a blue-and-white husky dog-girl character, like a hand-drawn Japanese character memo page. The theme is {argument name="character name" default="Husky"}, with a wintery blue ink sketchbook aesthetic, paw prints, hearts, sparkles, handwritten notes, and watercolor splashes.

Canvas: Portrait poster, roughly 3:4 aspect ratio, white paper background with blue watercolor stains and thin blue panel borders. Use a clean character-sheet layout with a large Japanese brush-calligraphy title at the top reading {argument name="main title" default="はすきーの特殊表情集"}, and a small English subtitle below it: “Special Expression Sheet”.

Character design: A young anime kemonomimi girl with large fluffy husky ears, very long messy {argument name="hair color" default="deep cobalt blue hair"}, blue ribbons and paw-shaped hair ornaments, a fluffy white fur-trimmed blue winter outfit, snowflake patterns, a blue bow at the chest, and a round paw-print pendant. Her overall palette is navy, ice blue, white, and soft gray. Draw her face clearly in each expression card; do not obscure it. Keep the same character design consistent across all panels.

Top area: On the upper left, include one tilted “Character Memo” box with a paw icon and five short Japanese-style profile lines: name, species, personality, likes, and special trait. On the upper right, include one small profile badge/card with the text “はすきー / HUSKY”, a tiny bust portrait of the character, paw prints, and small handwritten notes.

Main layout: Use exactly 6 large rounded expression cards arranged in a 3-column by 2-row grid. Each card has a blue numbered badge, a large Japanese expression title, a smaller English translation, a short Japanese note line, and decorative handwritten sound effects. The six expression cards are:
1. “尊い” with English label “Blessed” — the character clasps her hands near her chest, overwhelmed with joy and tenderness, surrounded by pink hearts and soft sparkles.
2. “しょんぼり” with English label “Sad Puppy” — her husky ears droop low, shoulders slumped, hands gripping the outfit, with gloomy vertical lines and a small sad thought bubble.
3. “ブチギレ” with English label “Furious” — intense angry pose with clenched fists thrust forward, red anger marks, speed lines, and bold growling sound effects.
4. “てれてれ” with English label “Blushing” — shy bashful pose, head tilted, one hand near face, pink hearts and embarrassed handwritten marks.
5. “困惑” with English label “Confused” — uncertain expression, hands held close to chest, question marks, spiral symbols, and hesitant side notes.
6. “どやっ” with English label “Smug” — confident smug pose with one hand near chin, sparkles, a small crown doodle, and proud handwritten sound effects.

Bottom strip: Add three small information sections. Left: one pinned “Designer’s Note” memo card with blue handwriting and a tiny heart signature. Center: one “Color Palette” strip with exactly 6 labeled swatches: snow white, ice blue, cobalt blue, aqua, navy, and gray. Right: one pinned “Favorite Things” memo card listing exactly 3 items: snow play, delicious food, and a favorite toy, with small icons such as a snowflake, bone, and toy capsule.

Visual style: Highly detailed anime illustration, soft watercolor and colored-pencil texture, blue ballpoint pen linework, hand-drawn notebook decorations, Japanese kawaii stationery design, light paper grain, ornate but readable layout. Use mostly blue ink with small pink accents only for hearts/blush and red accents only for anger marks.

Constraints: Keep exactly 6 expression panels, exactly 6 color swatches, exactly 3 favorite-things items, and one character memo box. Make all panel titles and numbers legible. No photorealism, no 3D render, no watermark, no extra panels, no unrelated characters.
title: Husky Girl Expression Sheet
description: A structured prompt for generating a detailed anime husky-girl special expression sheet with six labeled emotion panels and character memo details.

content:
Goal: Create a polished Japanese proposal-style idea sheet for a casual mobile puzzle game where players collect 3 matching pies to clear them, titled {argument name="headline text" default="パイを3つ集めて消す カジュアルゲーム 4テーマ案"}. The sheet should look like a cheerful game design concept board, with cute UI mockups and explanatory diagrams.

Canvas: Wide landscape 16:9 presentation sheet, warm cream background, thin rounded border, tiny sparkles and dessert decorations. Use a soft pastel kawaii illustration style with clean vector-like edges, subtle shadows, glossy game UI highlights, and high readability.

Layout: Divide the canvas into exactly 4 large rounded theme panels arranged in a 2×2 grid. Each panel has a numbered badge, a Japanese theme title banner, a smartphone gameplay mockup on the left, and instruction/example cards on the right. Add a bottom strip with exactly 4 short feature notes and icons.

Main title: At the top center, show a small pie icon followed by the large headline. Emphasize the number “4” in pink and add a small heart at the end.

Panel count and themes: Use exactly 4 theme concepts.
1. Pink dessert cafe theme titled {argument name="theme 1 title" default="ストロベリースイーツカフェ"}, with strawberry shortcakes, berry tarts, whipped cream, pink trays, and a cute bunny cafe mascot.
2. Yellow picnic theme titled {argument name="theme 2 title" default="アニマルピクニック"}, with a red-checkered picnic cloth, wicker basket tray, small animal friends, fruit pies, and sunny outdoor greenery.
3. Green fantasy woodland theme titled {argument name="theme 3 title" default="フェアリーフォレストベーカリー"}, with a magical forest bakery, mushrooms, lanterns, fairy-like character, berry pies, wooden tray, vines, leaves, and warm glowing lights.
4. Blue ocean dessert theme titled {argument name="theme 4 title" default="オーシャンデザートパーティー"}, with underwater pastel scenery, coral, shells, sea creatures, shell-shaped desserts, and a shell-decorated tray.

Smartphone mockups: Each of the 4 panels must include exactly 1 vertical phone screen on the left, for a total of 4 phones. Every phone shows a cute match-3-like pie collection game screen with a top HUD showing “Lv.15”, score “2,480”, and a pause button. In each game board, show many individual pies on a themed background, plus a bottom deck/tray with empty slots and a white hand cursor tapping or dragging a pie. Include a small yellow arrow indicating movement toward the tray.

Instruction cards: In each of the 4 panels, include a top explanation card headed “遊び方” with exactly 3 illustrated steps from left to right: tap a pie and put it into the deck, collect 3 of the same pie to clear them, and if the deck becomes full it is GAME OVER. Below it, include exactly 2 smaller example cards: one green/positive card labeled “3つそろえて消える例” showing 3 matching pies clearing with sparkles, and one red/warning card labeled “デッキがいっぱいの例” showing a full tray and the text “GAME OVER” with an exclamation warning icon.

Bottom strip: Add exactly 4 feature notes separated across the bottom, each with one icon: a hand icon with “かんたん操作 / タップだけのシンプル操作！”, a lightbulb icon with “3つそろえる爽快感！ / そろえるとパッと消えて気持ちいい♪”, a heart icon with “かわいいパイがいっぱい！ / 見ているだけで癒される4つの世界♪”, and a warning icon with “デッキが満杯になる前に / うまくそろえてハイスコアを目指そう！”.

Visual constraints: Keep all Japanese text crisp and legible, use rounded friendly fonts, maintain a consistent proposal-board layout, avoid photorealism, avoid dark or gritty styling, avoid extra panels, avoid extra phones, and do not add watermarks or logos.
title: Four Theme Pie Puzzle Game Sheet
description: Generates a cute Japanese proposal-style concept sheet for a casual mobile pie-matching game with four themed UI mockups.

content: Create an ultra-detailed sketchbook-style character exploration page on a pure bright white background, using my uploaded photo as a STRICT facial reference with absolute identity preservation. The character must unmistakably retain my exact facial structure, with zero facial drift, zero beautification, and no reinterpretation of identity. The page should look like an illustrator’s personal sketchbook filled organically over time, not like a clean or organized character sheet. Arrange the drawings in a spontaneous, overlapping, scattered composition with varying sizes and angles, as though an artist casually filled the page with ideas, expressions, experiments, notes, and visual jokes. Include a dynamic mix of: expressive full-body sketches, face close-ups, rough construction sketches, loose gesture drawings, tiny doodles, exaggerated reaction faces, chibi/deformed mini versions, casual pose studies, humorous scribbles, motion sketches, emotional expression tests, profile and three-quarter face angles, playful character exaggerations, unfinished line-art fragments, layered redraws and crossed-out sketch attempts. The overall art style should feel raw, energetic, intelligent, and highly expressive, with confident pencil strokes, messy line weight variation, rough ink marks, light shading, accidental smudges, handwritten notes, tiny arrows, annotations, and sketchbook imperfections. Preserve a handmade traditional animation-development feel. Visually interpret the character as: highly creative and analytical, tech-oriented and engineering-minded, observant and skeptical, quietly confident, humorous in a dry, understated way, stylish without trying too hard, mentally sharp but slightly chaotic from overthinking ideas, someone constantly experimenting, refining, and building things. The clothing designs should blend modern casual fashion with subtle creative-tech aesthetics: relaxed streetwear, {argument name="clothing theme" default="football-inspired outfits"}, layered jackets or hoodies, clean sneakers, bracelets or small accessories, occasional {argument name="branding" default="Arsenal-inspired"} visual elements, casual poses that feel natural and self-assured. Some sketches should look polished while others remain rough and incomplete, creating the feeling of an authentic working sketchbook page. content:
Create a three-view drawing of a {argument name="robot type" default="humanoid robot"} to be operated at a lunar base.
Display three views—front, side, and back—arranged side-by-side at the same scale and with the same design.
The background should be white or very light gray, creating a clean three-view diagram for reference material.

The design should be a {argument name="design style" default="sharp, high-detail Japanese real-robot-style mecha design"}.
It should not be too slender, but have a convincing appearance of a practical machine, with an atmosphere somewhere between military and industrial use.
Please do not make it look like a Gundam.
Keep it as an original lunar base robot.

Robot Features:
- Bipedal humanoid robot.
- Small head with a precision-machined sensor visor or monocular-style sensor.
- The face should not be too human-like, but inorganic and intelligent.
- Panel lines and segmented structures on the shoulders, chest, waist, and legs for a sense of precision.
- Legs should have a solid joint structure with a sense of stability suitable for lunar movement.
- Arms should be slightly long, with 5-finger manipulators for hands.
- Equipped with a backpack or rear unit with structures suggesting functions for communication, power, and attitude control.
- Excessive weaponry is not required.
- Do not give it weapons.
- A practical robot used for patrols, security, auxiliary work, and extravehicular activity support at the lunar base.

Design Taste:
- {argument name="color scheme" default="Based on white, light gray, and dark gray"}.
- Accent colors of modest red or orange, with a tiny bit of blue-ish accents.
- A clean color scheme that harmonizes with spacesuits and lunar equipment.
- Persuasiveness as an industrial product.
- A sharp-edged silhouette.
- Include some fine details, precise mechanisms, panel lines, and cautionary markings.
- Futuristic feel, but a realistic and grounded mechanical expression.

Requirements for the Three-View Drawing:
- Clearly separate the front, side, and back views.
- Ensure the full body is within the frame without being cut off.
- The pose should be a neutral, upright standing pose.
- Ensure the differences in shape are clear in each view.
- Emphasize informative/reference value.
- No unnecessary background effects.
- No explosions, action, or smoke.
- Presented as setting reference material rather than a character illustration.

Canvas: Wide 16:9 contact-sheet layout, 1280x720 style, divided into a clean 5-column by 3-row grid. Each panel has a thin white border, a blue circular number badge in the top-left, a white slanted title banner across the top with bold blue uppercase English text, and a dark translucent caption bar along the bottom with Japanese explanatory text.

Setting and visual style: Sunny outdoor football stadium with green field, running track, curved grandstands, vivid blue sky, fluffy clouds, energetic idol-cheer atmosphere. High-quality anime illustration, glossy promotional look, dynamic wide-angle camera, bright rim light, saturated blue-and-white palette, crisp details, motion in hair and skirt. The character wears a blue-and-white cheerleader outfit: cropped sleeveless top with star motif, pleated mini skirt, blue ribbons, thigh garter accents, ankle shoes, and large blue-and-white pom-poms. Long flowing {argument name="hair color" default="silver-white"} twin-tail hair with blue bows, cute expressive idol style.

Layout and exact panel list: Include exactly 15 panels, ordered left to right, top to bottom:
1. HIGH V — both arms raised high in a V shape with pom-poms.
2. LOW V — both arms extended diagonally downward in a low V stance.
3. T MOTION — arms stretched straight sideways at shoulder height.
4. TOUCHDOWN — one arm raised high and the other extended diagonally downward.
5. HIGH TOUCH — one hand reaches straight upward as if touching high, one leg lifted playfully.
6. LOW TOUCH — leaning forward with one arm reaching low toward the ground.
7. HALF TIME — hands placed on hips, ready stance.
8. CLAP — pom-poms brought together in front, clapping rhythmically.
9. PUNCH — one arm thrust powerfully upward with pom-pom.
10. SPIN — spinning in place, skirt and hair flaring outward.
11. KICK — high leg kick with energetic balance.
12. LUNGE — one knee bent deeply in a strong lunge pose.
13. JUMP — jumping upward with pom-pom raised and legs tucked.
14. BOW — polite forward bow with pom-poms gathered in front.
15. FINISH POSE — final celebratory pose with one arm raised and smile.

Text content: Use the English motion titles exactly as listed. Add Japanese captions similar to a cheer instruction guide under each panel, including these meanings: basic high V position, calm low V stance, synchronized shoulder-height team motion, one hand high and one diagonal, touch high to appeal height, low touch with sparkle, hands on waist for preparation, clap pom-poms to make rhythm, punch upward with strength, spin for華やか movement, dynamic high kick, strong lunge, jump to express energy, graceful bow of thanks, and smiling finish pose. Main title is not needed.

Constraints: Keep the grid perfectly readable and evenly spaced. Use exactly 15 numbered panels and no extra panels. Maintain consistent character design, outfit, colors, and stadium background across all panels. Make the typography bold and legible. Avoid logos, watermarks, and unrelated objects.

content: Create a cinematic vertical cyberpunk photorealistic scene set inside a late-night subway train during heavy rain. In the foreground, a hand with long glossy black-and-pink stiletto nails holds a large black smartphone angled toward the camera. The phone displays a dark music player interface for {argument name="song title" default="Midnight Reverie"} by {argument name="artist name" default="LUNA"}; the album cover shows the same gothic woman sitting in the train, with her face intentionally covered by a dark square. The phone UI includes exactly 11 visible interface elements: the time 23:47, the small playlist title “Night Drive,” one album cover image, the song title, the artist name, one pink heart icon, one pink progress bar with timestamps 1:53 and 4:08, five main playback controls arranged in a row with a large pink pause button in the center, and four small bottom icons. In the midground to the right, the real woman from the album cover sits on the subway bench, wearing a glossy black latex gothic outfit with choker, buckles, straps, long sleeves, thigh straps, and high-shine black styling; she has {argument name="hair style" default="short messy black bob"}, silver earrings, and her face is covered by a dark square. Outside the large train window is a rainy neon city at night with wet reflections, purple and electric-blue lighting, skyscrapers, glowing Japanese and cyberpunk signs, and streaks of rain on the glass. Inside the train, show metal poles, dark seats, ceiling lights, graffiti, posters, and a few distant passengers; one passenger on the left is looking at a phone and has a blurred or obscured face. Use a moody {argument name="color palette" default="deep violet, magenta, electric blue, and black"} palette, shallow depth of field, realistic reflections on latex and glass, rain-soaked atmosphere, and the feeling of riding the last train while listening to music. No watermark, no extra captions outside the phone UI, no extra people in the foreground.

description: A cinematic cyberpunk rain-night subway scene with a smartphone music player matching the gothic passenger in the train.

content:
Goal: Create a single-page fantasy character sheet for {argument name="character name" default="Sera Vayne"}, a tide-priestess warrior with a ceremonial war glaive, elegant coastal mysticism, and high-detail concept art presentation.

Canvas: Wide landscape character design board, approximately 16:9, warm off-white parchment background with thin grey panel borders, painterly ink-and-watercolor fantasy concept art, refined but slightly weathered. Use a mostly neutral cream, deep blue-black, pale ivory, seafoam teal, warm tan, and bright bioluminescent blue palette.

Main hero pose: On the left third, show a full-body heroic standing pose of a barefoot adult woman with tanned skin, athletic slender build, long wild black wavy hair tied loosely back, layered necklaces, shell and bead ornaments, dangling earrings, and a calm ritual-warrior presence. She wears a pale translucent salt-white halter wrap dress with a high slit, a seafoam waist sash, wrapped forearm bandages, anklets, and a flowing tattered deep blue-black cloak that looks like wet tide fabric. She holds a tall ornate ceremonial glaive upright beside her; the blade is jagged, elegant, dark metal with glowing blue edges and carved wave-like ornament. Add subtle water-splash stains and ink textures around her hem.

Left title column: Add four large vertical Chinese characters down the far left with small English translations beside them: tide, silence, blade, body, return. Include a small red seal stamp beneath. At the bottom left, write the large brush-lettered name {argument name="character name" default="Sera Vayne"}, subtitle {argument name="subtitle" default="The Rite Before the Tide"}, and three small lore lines: ROLE: War Priestess · Tide Reader · Last of the Coastal Order; CORE THEME: The sea does not rage — it arrives. She moves the same way. Patient, total, final.; WEAPON: Tidecaller — ceremonial war glaive. The blade glows because the tide chose her.

Orthographic views: In the upper middle column, include exactly 3 labeled views: 1 FRONT full-body view with the glaive standing beside her, 2 BACK full-body view showing the cloak and hair from behind, 3 PROFILE side view showing her carrying the weapon. Use small star-like label markers and uppercase labels FRONT, BACK, PROFILE.

Expression sheet: In the upper right, create a boxed section titled EXPRESSIONS containing exactly 6 head-and-shoulder portraits in a 3-by-2 grid. Labels must be: 1 Ceremonial neutral, 2 Focused pre-strike, 3 Profile left, 4 Post-release, 5 Looking down, 6 3/4 right. Keep facial features consistent, hair loose and wet-textured, jewelry visible, and the same costume neckline.

Costume detail insets: In the center-right, create a boxed DETAILS section with exactly 4 vertical close-up panels labeled: 1 Waist & hip, 2 Neckline & collarbone, 3 Leg & hem, 4 Forearm & hand. Show fabric layering, shells, beads, sash knots, translucent cloth, tattered cloak edges, barefoot ankle jewelry, and wrapped forearm details.

Signature prop breakdown: On the right side, create a SIGNATURE PROP section showing the glaive diagonally from lower left to upper right, with callout arrows and labels for exactly 6 parts: blade, glow edge, engraving, grip wrap, shaft, butt cap. Add a small zoom-in box of the blade tip emphasizing carved metal and blue luminous edge.

Silhouette study row: Along the bottom center, include exactly 6 black silhouette thumbnails labeled: 1 Neutral standing, 2 Low sweep prep, 3 Overhead raise, 4 Aerial extension, 5 Kneeling finish, 6 Horizon facing. Make the silhouettes readable, dynamic, and varied, always including the long glaive and flowing cloak.

Color palette: On the lower right, include a COLOR PALETTE box with exactly 6 circular swatches and labels: 1 Deep Ocean Blue-Black — Ocean blue-black: ritual authority, depth, command; 2 Pale Salt White — Salt white: endurance, tide foam, worn grace; 3 Seafoam Teal with Gold — Seafoam teal: water connection, ceremonial rank; 4 Warm Tan — Warm Tan: grounded physicality, natural strength; 5 Bioluminescent Blue — Bioluminescent blue: the tide’s mark, living power; 6 muted shell-gold accent for ornaments and ceremonial details.

Style constraints: Make it look like a professional AAA fantasy character design sheet, painterly semi-realistic illustration, crisp section labels, thin panel lines, no extra characters, no modern objects, no UI mockup elements, no photorealistic studio lighting, no clutter beyond the specified sections.
title: Fantasy Tide Priestess Character Sheet
description: Generates a complete fantasy character design sheet with hero pose, orthographic views, expressions, costume details, weapon breakdown, silhouettes, and palette.

content:
Goal: Create a wide anime-style isekai reincarnation diagnosis result card set in a luminous steampunk fantasy harbor. The image should feel like a collectible visual novel result screen, with ornate typography, clockwork motifs, and a sense that a story is about to begin.

Canvas: Horizontal 16:9 illustration, cinematic composition, very high detail, pastel blue sky and sea, warm sunlight, crisp anime rendering.

Layout: Place a huge ornate Japanese title across the upper left: 「潮騒の歯車港」 with gold trim, blue gradient fill, decorative gears, star ornaments, swirls, and nautical-clock accents. Directly below it place the subtitle 「異世界転生 診断」 and a small English line “ISEKAI REBIRTH RESULT”. On the lower left, place one dark teal translucent information panel with a thin gold ornamental frame, small gears, corner decorations, and subtle horizontal dividers. On the right half, place the main character large in the foreground, seated or leaning among papers and clockwork parts, with a square flat skin-tone placeholder covering the face area for later customization.

Main character: A young anime girl named {argument name="character name" default="のぞむ"}, with soft brown shoulder-length hair blowing in the wind, delicate hairpins, ribbons, a white beret-like sailor hat with a small rose decoration, frilly white blouse, pale blue dress, black corset waist, brown ribbon bow at the chest, lace cuffs, gold chains, small gears, compass jewelry, and a pocket-watch accessory. She poses gracefully with one hand near her hair/hat, surrounded by fluttering white papers and ribbons. Keep the face hidden by one centered opaque square placeholder in muted peach color.

Background: A vast fantasy harbor city named {argument name="world name" default="潮騒の歯車港"}, filled with turquoise canals, bridges, piers, white stone buildings, elegant towers, clock faces, giant gears, airship-like structures, seagulls, sails, and a bright blue sky with fluffy clouds. Include multiple brass clock mechanisms and nautical instruments on the right foreground, including a large circular gear/clock frame.

Information panel text: Use exactly 8 visible labeled entries inside the left panel. 1) 「名前： のぞむ」. 2) 「世界名： 潮騒の歯車港」. 3) 「役職： 潮路の案内人」. 4) 「世界適性： S Rank」 with “S Rank” large in gold serif letters. 5) 「帰還難易度： SS」 with “SS” large in pink-red letters. 6) 「最初のクエスト： 止まった港時計を動かす」. 7) 「運命ログ： この港は、あなたが来るのをずっと待っていた。」. 8) Bottom status line split into two small items: 「祝福率：98%」 and 「危険度：★★★☆☆」.

Visual style: Intricate Japanese light novel cover art, dreamy romantic steampunk, detailed linework, soft glow, sparkling highlights, ornate gold filigree, airy composition, high-resolution illustration. Use a color palette of sky blue, sea teal, ivory white, brass gold, blush pink, and warm brown.

Constraints: Keep all text readable and placed as described. Do not add extra result rows beyond the 8 listed entries. Do not add watermarks, logos, QR codes, or modern UI elements. Maintain the face-covering square placeholder and the fantasy harbor diagnosis-result composition.
title: Steampunk Isekai Rebirth Result Card
description: Generates an ornate anime fantasy harbor diagnosis-result illustration with Japanese isekai title typography, a character placeholder, and a detailed result panel.

Goal: Create a clean pixel-art sprite sheet for a fantasy game asset: {argument name="character concept" default="a medieval knight riding a winged donkey"}.

Canvas: Square 768×768 image on a plain white background, no borders, no UI, no text. Use a crisp retro RPG pixel-art style with visible chunky pixels, dark outlines, limited palette, and subtle dithering/shading.

Layout: Show exactly 8 separate sprites of the same mount-and-rider character, evenly spaced with large white gaps, arranged as 3 sprites on the top row, 2 sprites on the middle row, and 3 sprites on the bottom row. Each sprite should be roughly the same scale and read like an animation/rotation sheet.

Sprite count and views: 1) top-left: rear three-quarter view facing upper-left, 2) top-center: straight rear view, 3) top-right: rear three-quarter view facing upper-right, 4) middle-left: side view traveling left, 5) middle-right: side view traveling right, 6) bottom-left: front three-quarter view facing lower-left, 7) bottom-center: straight front view, 8) bottom-right: front three-quarter view facing lower-right.

Subject details: The mount is a small gray-brown donkey with long upright ears, pale muzzle, black hooves, simple bridle and saddle, and a pair of white feathered wings extended from its sides like a small pegasus. The rider is a compact armored knight wearing a rounded steel helmet with vertical visor slits, a bright red plume, a red cape or cloth drape, brown leather tack, and a blue shield with a gold cross emblem strapped at the side. Use {argument name="armor color" default="steel gray"}, {argument name="cape color" default="red"}, {argument name="shield color" default="blue and gold"}, and {argument name="mount color" default="gray-brown"}. Maintain the same proportions and costume details across all 8 sprites.

Visual style: Classic 16-bit fantasy RPG sprite sheet, isometric/top-down compatible proportions, high readability at small size, black and dark-brown pixel outlines, soft shadowless presentation, minimal anti-aliasing, no blur, no gradients beyond pixel shading.

Constraints: Keep exactly 8 sprites, do not add extra frames, labels, weapons, scenery, background texture, watermark, or character variations. All sprites must depict the same mounted knight and winged donkey from different directions.
title: Winged Donkey Knight Sprite Sheet
description: Generates an 8-direction retro pixel-art sprite sheet of a medieval knight riding a winged donkey for fantasy game assets.

content: Use the reference image as the base for character design. content:
Goal: Create a screenshot-like collage showing a pile of unusable AI-generated fictional world map attempts on a pale gray workspace, as if viewed on an image-generation history board. The subject is not one map, but a messy contact sheet of many map thumbnails for a future fictional nation.

Canvas: Wide 16:10 landscape canvas, light gray background with a faint dotted grid pattern. Leave generous gutters between thumbnails. The entire composition should feel like a zoomed-out browser or design-board view, with some thumbnails cut off by the right edge.

Layout: Arrange exactly 35 visible map thumbnails in an irregular grid. Count them as: 8 small thumbnails across the top row, 10 large landscape thumbnails in the middle two rows, 12 smaller portrait/landscape thumbnails in the lower middle arranged in paired columns, and 5 large purple-themed thumbnails along the bottom row. Include several partially cropped thumbnails on the far right edge to imply the board continues off-screen.

Thumbnail content: Each thumbnail is a different failed variation of a futuristic world map for {argument name="fictional nation name" default="United Federation of Valoria"}. Most maps show altered Earth-like continents but not an accurate real-world map. Use dark navy or black ocean backgrounds, neon blue coastlines, cyan glowing borders, faint green landmasses, satellite-map textures, grid overlays, small HUD-like ticks, and occasional white title text. Some thumbnails should look like color tests: one bright blue-and-yellow terrain map, one simple black-outline map on white, and multiple glowing cyberpunk versions. The bottom row should use a different purple faction label, {argument name="bottom faction name" default="Empathia Union"}, with purple-highlighted landmasses around Eurasia/Australia-like shapes.

Visible labels: Repeat the title text on many thumbnails, usually centered or near the lower third, reading {argument name="main map label" default="United Federation of Valoria"}. Vary the typography and size slightly so some labels are tiny, misaligned, or awkwardly placed. Bottom-row thumbnails should instead read {argument name="purple map label" default="Empathia Union"}. A few labels may be partially illegible due to scale, cropping, or glow.

Visual style: Make it look like a collection of generated image results, not a polished infographic. Use inconsistent map compositions, inconsistent coastline shapes, different zoom levels, different title placements, and slightly mismatched aspect ratios. The dominant palette is black, deep navy, electric cyan, blue, green, and purple, with occasional white thumbnails and one or two bright terrain-map thumbnails.

Constraints: Do not create a single clean world map. Do not make the continents match the real Earth exactly. Do not add people, UI controls, large captions, watermarks, or a polished final design. Preserve the feeling of many discarded attempts scattered on a pale dotted canvas.
title: Fictional World Map Attempts Collage
description: Generates a zoomed-out contact sheet of many failed futuristic fictional world map variations for creative worldbuilding.

Room architecture: One arched alcove room with a vaulted gothic ceiling, carved black wood frame, dark bronze trim, twisted vine-like columns on both left and right front edges, dark parquet floor, and a rounded front platform edge. The palette is nearly black, espresso brown, antique gold, dark emerald, smoke gray, and tiny amber highlights.

Decorative details: Use draped chains, bead garlands, lace-like canopy curtains, tiny potion bottles, a glass decanter, teacups, a kettle, stacked books, carved filigree, a circular dark green rug with gold embroidery, stained-glass arched window on the upper left, fish-scale or net-pattern wall panel on the right, and a snake motif carved into the left cabinet door. Constraints: Do not add readable text, labels, logos, or watermarks. Keep the composition symmetrical and enclosed, with the bed and 2 dolls as the emotional center. content:
Goal: Create a realistic 3D character reference sheet for a cute plush-like creature named {argument name="character name" default="bioluminescent cream forest cub"}, designed for consistent video generation.

Canvas: Vertical 4:5 character sheet on a matte medium-gray studio background, soft diffused lighting, subtle floor shadows, high-resolution photorealistic fur rendering.

Subject details: The character is a small chubby teddy-bear/cat hybrid with a round oversized head, short rounded body, tiny arms, stubby legs, cream-colored dense fuzzy fur, glossy dark olive-brown eyes with bright catchlights, a tiny dark brown nose and mouth, faint pink cheeks, one thin curled hair sprout on top of the head, small rounded ears that glow bioluminescent green inside, and a short tail with a glowing green tip. The overall mood is adorable, soft, and toy-like, with realistic fiber texture and gentle translucency in the glowing green areas. Use {argument name="fur color" default="warm cream"}, {argument name="glow color" default="bioluminescent green"}, and {argument name="cheek color" default="soft pink blush"}.

Layout: Arrange the sheet in two main rows. Top row contains exactly 3 full-body turnaround views, evenly spaced and labeled in small white uppercase text: 1) “01 FRONT” showing the creature facing forward, 2) “02 3/4 LEFT” showing a three-quarter left view with the tail visible behind, 3) “03 BACK” showing the back view with rounded head, back fur, small arms, legs, and glowing tail tip. Bottom left contains an expression sheet with exactly 3 vertical framed panels labeled “EXPRESSION SHEET” above and captions below: 1) “01 NEUTRAL” with calm forward-facing eyes and closed small mouth, 2) “02 BATTLE FURY” with the face area covered by a centered opaque beige square block, 3) “03 SHOCK” with raised ears, wide eyes, and open surprised mouth. Bottom right contains a color palette column labeled “COLOR PALETTE” with exactly 5 rectangular swatches and labels: 1) “CREAM FUR” beige fur texture, 2) “BIOLUMINESCENT GREEN” glowing green gradient, 3) “PINK CHEEK” rosy pink texture, 4) “DARK BROWN OUTLINE / NOSE” dark brown swatch, 5) “WHITE HIGHLIGHT” white swatch.

Text style: Small clean sans-serif uppercase white labels, minimal technical character-sheet typography, aligned precisely and unobtrusively.

Constraints: Keep exactly 3 turnaround views, exactly 3 expression panels, and exactly 5 color swatches. Do not add extra characters, props, logos, watermarks, or decorative background elements. Maintain consistent proportions, fur texture, glowing ears and tail, and facial design across all views.

description: Generates a realistic character turnaround and expression sheet for a cute glowing plush creature used in consistent animation or video workflows.

title: Cinematic Character Design Board
description: A prompt for creating a professional character design board for film, including full-body turnarounds and facial studies.

content:
[Layout]
Configure as a single, organized character sheet. White or very light gray background. Clean layout with easy-to-read margins. Each panel should have a dark blue or calm blue heading label. Minimal decoration, prioritizing clarity of information.

[Required Panel Configuration]
1. Full Body (Front)
2. Full Body (Side)
3. Full Body (Back)
4. Face Close-up (Front)
5. Face Close-up (45-degree angle)
6. Face Close-up (Profile)
7. Upper Body Close-up (Front)

[Content for Each Panel]
- Full Body (Front): A natural standing pose. Make face, hair, entire suit, skirt length, black high socks, and shoes visible.
- Full Body (Side): Profile standing pose. Make the side profile of the face, chest, waist, hips, leg lines, hair fall, and twintail position visible.
- Full Body (Back): Rear view. Show the parting, how the twintails look from the back, jacket back, skirt back, and leg lines.
- Face Close-up (Front): Reference shot of the face. Clearly show eyes, nose, mouth, contour, bangs, and ribbon position.
- Face Close-up (45-degree angle): Show the three-dimensionality of the face from an angle, appearance of cheeks, fall of bangs, and one twintail.
- Face Close-up (Profile): Clearly show the forehead, bridge of the nose, mouth, chin, ear position, neck connection, and side profile silhouette.
- Upper Body Close-up (Front): In addition to the face, show the jacket collar, blouse collar, shoulder shape, and silhouette from the chest to the upper waist.

[Style/Texture]
High-quality portrait expression leaning towards photorealism. Avoid excessive AI-style skin processing; keep a natural skin feel for a {argument name="subject" default="young person"}. Skin should be clean and smooth but not wax-like. {argument name="hair" default="Black hair"} should have a natural shine. The {argument name="outfit" default="suit"} should have a clean, high-quality fabric feel. Overall bright, clear, and highly informative finish.

[Excluded Items]
No tiny costume detail windows. No color palette column. No long profile section. No excessive explanatory text. No scene, background, or prop staging.

[Prohibitions]
Changing the person, making them look younger, mass-produced anime face, oversized eyes, unstable nose or chin shape, broken side profiles, changing twintail positions, changing ribbon shapes, altered hairstyles, suit color changes, unnecessary accessories, excessive skin beautification, excessive shadows, or cluttered layouts.
title: Professional Character Sheet Template
description: A comprehensive structured prompt for generating professional character reference sheets with multiple angles and close-ups to ensure character consistency.

content: Create a highly detailed fantasy cyberpunk cityscape at night titled {argument name="scene concept" default="Neon Dream City"}: a romantic canal city with Venetian architecture fused with futuristic glass towers, glowing magenta and cyan lights, and a luminous full moon in a deep blue starry sky.
